page.title: Marbella Family Snorkeling & Paddleboard Boat Combo Experience

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella family snorkeling paddleboard boat combo from Puerto Banús. 12+ boats, 5-12 pax, skipper & drinks included. Fun on the Costa del Sol.
data.summary: This combo experience combines family-friendly snorkeling and paddleboarding with a private boat charter from Puerto Banús. Choose from 12+ boats (5-12 pax) with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Snorkeling gear and paddleboards provided on board
Skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT included
Soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, and snacks included
Popular spots: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h with jet ski

faq.q: What boats are available for the family snorkeling and paddleboard combo?
faq.a: We offer over 12 boats departing from Puerto Banús, ranging from 5 to 12 passengers. Popular choices include the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax). All come with a licensed skipper and full amenities.

faq.q: Is snorkeling gear provided, or do I need to bring my own?
faq.a: Snorkeling gear (masks, fins, and snorkels) is provided on board at no extra cost. Paddleboards are also included. Just bring your swimwear, sunscreen, and a towel.

faq.q: How long is the experience, and what are the prices?
faq.a: Charters start from 2 hours. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) have a 4-hour minimum at €4,719 and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in the price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Where do we go for snorkeling and paddleboarding?
faq.a: Your skipper will choose the best spots based on weather and sea conditions. Popular options include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the clear waters near Cabopino. You can also anchor off the Golden Mile or explore the coast toward Estepona.

faq.q: Is this suitable for young children?
faq.a: Yes, the experience is family-friendly. Boats have safety equipment and life jackets for all ages. The shallow, calm coves are ideal for kids. Paddleboards are stable and easy to use.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat has a fridge and coolbox. Our included drinks and snacks are enough for most, but extras are fine.

faq.q: How do I book, and what is the cancellation policy?
faq.a: Book via our WhatsApp number or through the website. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due on the day. Cancellations up to 48 hours before departure are fully refundable. Within 48 hours, the deposit is non-refundable unless we cancel due to weather.

data.body_html:
Combine the best of both worlds on a Marbella family snorkeling paddleboard boat charter. You get a private boat from Puerto Banús, with all the gear for snorkeling and paddleboarding, plus a skipper who knows the best coves. No crowds, no queues—just your family and the Mediterranean. Choose from 12+ boats (5 to 12 passengers), all with drinks, snacks, and insurance included.

Why choose a snorkeling and paddleboard combo?

Snorkeling and paddleboarding are the two easiest ways to enjoy the Costa del Sol's clear waters. You don't need experience—just a swimsuit and sunscreen. The combo lets you switch between exploring underwater life and gliding on the surface. Kids love both, and adults get to relax on deck between dips. Our skippers anchor in calm coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, where the water is shallow and safe.

Our boats for families

We have over 12 boats departing from Puerto Banús. For smaller families, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers more space and even includes a free jet ski on 4-hour+ bookings. All boats have shaded areas, a fridge, and a toilet. See our full fleet on the Marbella boat rental page.

Pricing and durations

Charters start at 2 hours. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) have a 4-hour minimum at €4,719 and include a jet ski. Other boats (e.g., Fairline Targa 12m, Pershing 46) are priced on request via WhatsApp. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

What's included?

  • Licensed skipper (speaks English and Spanish)
  • Fuel for the full charter
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (chips, nuts, olives)
  • Snorkeling masks, fins, and snorkels
  • Paddleboards (1-2 depending on boat size)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

No hidden extras. Just bring your towel and sunscreen.

Best spots for snorkeling and paddleboarding

Your skipper will choose the day's best locations. Popular stops include:

  • Cala del Faro – a sheltered cove with rocky reefs and small fish
  • Cala Cortés – clear water and a sandy beach
  • Cabopino – sand dunes and calm shallows
  • Río Verde – deeper water for more adventurous snorkelers

If you prefer a longer trip, you can head toward Estepona or Sotogrande. The skipper will tailor the route to your preferences.

How to book

Booking is easy. Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, boat, and number of guests. We'll confirm availability and send a payment link for the 50% deposit. The balance is due on the day. For more details, check our Marbella boat rental page or read about family experiences.

Frequently asked questions

What boats are available for the family snorkeling and paddleboard combo?

We offer over 12 boats departing from Puerto Banús, ranging from 5 to 12 passengers. Popular choices include the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), and Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax). All come with a licensed skipper and full amenities.

Is snorkeling gear provided, or do I need to bring my own?

Snorkeling gear (masks, fins, and snorkels) is provided on board at no extra cost. Paddleboards are also included. Just bring your swimwear, sunscreen, and a towel.

How long is the experience, and what are the prices?

Charters start from 2 hours. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) have a 4-hour minimum at €4,719 and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in the price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

Where do we go for snorkeling and paddleboarding?

Your skipper will choose the best spots based on weather and sea conditions. Popular options include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the clear waters near Cabopino. You can also anchor off the Golden Mile or explore the coast toward Estepona.

Is this suitable for young children?

Yes, the experience is family-friendly. Boats have safety equipment and life jackets for all ages. The shallow, calm coves are ideal for kids. Paddleboards are stable and easy to use.

Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat has a fridge and coolbox. Our included drinks and snacks are enough for most, but extras are fine.

How do I book, and what is the cancellation policy?

Book via our WhatsApp number or through the website. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due on the day. Cancellations up to 48 hours before departure are fully refundable. Within 48 hours, the deposit is non-refundable unless we cancel due to weather.
